goal: RoboHive ADROIT 'relocate-pen' Isaac-Sim parity scene (parallel-jaw approximation): a Franka arm picks an elongated cylindrical pen (radius 0.008 m, height 0.15 m) lying horizontally on a tabletop and places it at a target tabletop location with the long axis re-oriented from horizontal (+X) to vertical (+Z). The pen is modeled as a Cylinder primitive (cylindrical surface chosen for ADROIT-relocate fidelity; cube-substitution would lose the rolling/orientation-ambiguity failure modes). define_grasp_pose registers a side-approach grasp aligned with the cylinder's long axis so the parallel jaws close perpendicular to that axis (best friction contact). setup_pick_place_controller with target_source='curobo' drives the pick + transport + drop, then move_to_pose executes a wrist roll that brings the pen to vertical at the target XY. Success criterion (reorient pattern): (a) pen center within 0.05 m of target_position AND (b) pen long-axis aligned with +Z within ~5 deg (computed from get_world_transform on the pen). Failure modes documented include cylindrical instability (pen rolls during transport — small contact patch + low friction), grasp slip on the curved surface, 2-fold symmetry around long axis (which 'up' counts as correct — the success metric uses axis-direction-cosine, not absolute roll, so this is acceptable), and tight-tolerance target pose conflict with cuRobo approach direction.
thoughts:
Design rationale for CP-NEW-robohive-relocate-pen (RoboHive ADROIT relocate-pen Isaac parity, parallel-jaw substitution):

1) Source benchmark: RoboHive's ADROIT relocate-pen task uses a 24-DOF dexterous hand picking a pen-shaped object and placing it at a target SE(3) pose. The ADROIT hand asset is Nucleus-only (asset_dependencies blocker in canonical_backlog.yaml entry research-robohive-relocate-pen-001), so this Isaac canonical substitutes the Franka parallel-jaw end-effector — preserving the elongated-workpiece reorient signature, dropping the dexterous-finger contact richness. The structural_tag isaac:research.robohive keeps the lineage; the failure_modes section explicitly documents the substitution boundary.

2) Workpiece geometry: Cylinder primitive r=0.008 m, h=0.15 m. Pen-sized: 16 mm diameter, 150 mm length, aspect ratio ~9:1. Mass set to 0.012 kg (12 g, typical ballpoint pen). The Cylinder prim type (Usd.Cylinder) gives a curved collision surface — critical because rolling instability is a real failure mode that wouldn't appear if we substituted a square-cross-section box (which is the easy cube-fallback). isaac:workpiece.elongated structural_tag captures the aspect-ratio signature for retrieval.

3) Scene layout: Franka at [0, 0, 0.75] on a 0.75 m table. Pen lies horizontally along world +X at table position [0.0, 0.40, 0.758] — long-axis horizontal, half-pen-length (0.075) in front of the Franka base, 0.008 m above table (cylinder radius). Target marker at [0.0, -0.40, 0.83] with long-axis vertical — this is target_position; the +0.072 lift above table accounts for half pen length when vertical (0.075) minus a 3 mm 'placed' settle.

4) Initial orientation: pen long axis along world +X. Cylinder primitives default to long-axis along +Z, so to lie horizontally along +X we rotate -90 deg about Y: quaternion [w, x, y, z] = [0.7071, 0, -0.7071, 0] (cos(-45deg), 0, sin(-45deg), 0). When the pen settles, gravity holds it on its side along +X.

5) Target orientation: pen long axis along world +Z (vertical, identity rotation for a Z-aligned Cylinder). Final quat = [1, 0, 0, 0]. The reorient delta is a -90 deg rotation about Y (inverse of the initial roll into vertical-up).

6) Grasp definition: define_grasp_pose with approach_direction=[0, 0, -1] (top-down onto the lying pen). gripper_offset=[0, 0, 0.01] — only 10 mm hover because the pen is so thin (16 mm diameter) that the Franka fingers must close almost to contact. The grasp YAML written to workspace/grasp_poses/Pen.isaac_grasp is advisory for downstream rmpflow/cuRobo; the controller uses planning_obstacles + drop_targets for actual motion, but define_grasp_pose records the side-approach intent in the artefact set. Key cylindrical-grasp consideration: parallel jaws must close perpendicular to the pen's long axis (the +X axis at pick time) so the contact line runs along the pen's curve — this is the friction-maximising orientation. Franka's default gripper Y axis aligns with the finger-closure direction; we want gripper_Y perpendicular to world_X at the grasp, so the EE orientation at the pick pose has gripper_Z=-world_Z and gripper_Y=±world_Y. cuRobo's grasp solver picks this automatically when given the cylinder's principal axis via planning_obstacles (the Cylinder prim carries its xformOp:orient).

7) Pick / transport / reorient / place: setup_pick_place_controller target_source='curobo' source_paths=['/World/Pen'] destination_path='/World/PenTarget' drop_targets={'/World/Pen': [0.0, -0.40, 0.83]} planning_obstacles=['/World/Table']. cuRobo transports the pen to the drop point while held by the gripper. Then move_to_pose(articulation_path='/World/Franka', target_position=[0.0, -0.40, 0.83], target_orientation=[0.7071, 0, 0.7071, 0], planner='rmpflow', robot_type='franka') executes the wrist roll: target_orientation [0.7071, 0, 0.7071, 0] is a +90 deg rotation about Y in the EE frame, which combined with the pen-held-in-grasp transform brings the pen long axis from +X (held by horizontal jaws) to +Z (vertical). After the reorient, the pen is at the target SE(3) pose. The controller's release phase (events 7-9) opens the gripper and lets the pen settle vertically.

8) Why move_to_pose AFTER the controller (not as part of the transport): cuRobo's setup_pick_place_controller treats grasp orientation as fixed (held by the gripper) through the transport — it does not natively reorient the grasped part. We sequence (a) pick + transport with fixed grasp orientation via curobo, (b) explicit reorient with move_to_pose at the target XY, (c) gripper release (handled inside the controller's release phase, which the post-reorient pose triggers via the sensor or duration timeout). This is the same composition pattern as CP-NEW-bin-picking-with-flip but without the mid-air flip waypoint — the reorient happens at the drop location, not in transit.

9) Why no proximity sensor: the success metric is a position+orientation check on the pen prim (read via get_world_transform), not a bin-arrival event. simulate_args.target_position and simulate_args.target_orientation_axis are evaluated post-place by the function-gate harness; no add_proximity_sensor is needed. This is a distinguishing 'open-tabletop relocate' shape vs the 'into-bin' shape that dominates the existing reorient templates.

10) Physics: CPU dynamics (enable_gpu_dynamics=False, MBP broadphase) for deterministic settling. enable_ccd=True on the pen — small radius (8 mm) + grasp/release transitions benefit from continuous collision detection (a 16 mm cylinder can tunnel through a 10 mm gripper finger gap at high velocities). sleepThreshold=0.0 so PhysX does not freeze the pen mid-transport. apply_physics_material 'plastic' for the pen (typical pen-shell friction coefficient ~0.4 against the rubber gripper pads).

11) Failure modes captured: (a) cylindrical instability — pen rolls on its side during transport if the controller imparts lateral acceleration; mitigation is to pre-grip firmly via define_grasp_pose's gripper_offset=[0,0,0.01] (close to surface) plus rubber finger material. (b) grasp slip on curved surface — finger contact patch on a 16 mm cylinder is ~2 mm wide line contact; Franka's 70 N grip force × friction coeff 0.4 gives 28 N hold, more than enough for 12 g pen × 9.81 = 0.12 N weight, but failure can occur if the gripper closes off-axis. (c) 2-fold symmetry — the pen has cylindrical symmetry around its long axis, so 'up' is ambiguous; success metric uses axis-direction cosine (dot product of pen long-axis with target_orientation_axis ≥ cos(5 deg) = 0.9962), not absolute quaternion comparison, which would falsely fail rolled-but-correct end-states. (d) target pose IK conflict — vertical pen at [0.0, -0.40, 0.83] places EE at z ≈ 0.83 + 0.10 (held pen extends 0.075 below gripper) ≈ 0.93, well within Franka reach but requires gripper pointing downward; if the reorient over-rotates, cuRobo may refuse the release pose. Mitigation is to chain a verification get_world_transform after the move_to_pose and fall back to a second move_to_pose with adjusted yaw.

12) Why cuRobo + rmpflow mixed planner stack: cuRobo for the pick (collision-aware planning around the table obstacle), rmpflow for the reorient (single-pose target, no environment obstacles after the lift). Mixing planners across segments is the same honest pattern as CP-NEW-bin-picking-with-flip; both controllers are listed under motion_controllers.untested (no function-gate run yet).
code:
# ── Scene skeleton ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
create_prim(prim_path="/World/DomeLight", prim_type="DomeLight")
set_attribute(prim_path="/World/DomeLight", attr_name="inputs:intensity", value=1000.0)
create_prim(prim_path="/World/Ground", prim_type="Cube", position=[0, 0, -0.5], scale=[20, 20, 1])
apply_api_schema(prim_path="/World/Ground", schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")
create_prim(prim_path="/World/Cell", prim_type="Xform")

# ── Tabletop platform ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
create_prim(prim_path="/World/Table", prim_type="Cube", position=[0, 0, 0.375], scale=[1.2, 0.8, 0.375])
apply_api_schema(prim_path="/World/Table", schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")

# ── Physics: CPU dynamics for deterministic cylindrical-roll behaviour ───────
set_physics_scene_config(config={"enable_gpu_dynamics": False, "broadphase_type": "MBP"})

# ── Franka arm ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
robot_wizard(
    robot_name="franka",
    dest_path="/World/Franka",
    position=[0, 0, 0.75],
    orientation=[1, 0, 0, 0],
)

# ── Pen workpiece: thin cylinder lying horizontally along +X ────────────────
# Cylinder default long-axis = +Z; to lie along +X we rotate -90 deg about Y.
# quat [w, x, y, z] = [cos(-45deg), 0, sin(-45deg), 0] = [0.7071, 0, -0.7071, 0]
# Pen rests on table at z = table_top (0.75) + cylinder radius (0.008) = 0.758.
create_prim(
    prim_path="/World/Pen",
    prim_type="Cylinder",
    position=[0.0, 0.40, 0.758],
)
set_attribute(prim_path="/World/Pen", attr_name="radius", value=0.008)
set_attribute(prim_path="/World/Pen", attr_name="height", value=0.15)
set_attribute(
    prim_path="/World/Pen",
    attr_name="xformOp:orient",
    value=[0.7071, 0, -0.7071, 0],
)
for api in ("PhysicsRigidBodyAPI", "PhysicsCollisionAPI", "PhysicsMassAPI", "PhysxRigidBodyAPI"):
    apply_api_schema(prim_path="/World/Pen", schema_name=api)
set_attribute(prim_path="/World/Pen", attr_name="physics:mass", value=0.012)

bulk_set_attribute(
    prim_paths=["/World/Pen"],
    attr="physxRigidBody:sleepThreshold",
    value=0.0,
)
bulk_set_attribute(
    prim_paths=["/World/Pen"],
    attr="physxRigidBody:enableCCD",
    value=True,
)
apply_physics_material(prim_path="/World/Pen", material_name="plastic")

# ── Target marker (Xform with target pose; used as drop_target reference) ────
# Vertical orientation at the target XY. Identity quat [1, 0, 0, 0] = pen along +Z.
create_prim(
    prim_path="/World/PenTarget",
    prim_type="Xform",
    position=[0.0, -0.40, 0.83],
)
set_attribute(
    prim_path="/World/PenTarget",
    attr_name="xformOp:orient",
    value=[1.0, 0.0, 0.0, 0.0],
)

# ── Grasp pose: side approach perpendicular to pen long axis ────────────────
# approach_direction=[0, 0, -1] (top-down onto lying pen). The parallel jaws
# close along the EE Y axis, which at this approach is perpendicular to the
# pen long axis (+X) — line contact along the cylinder curve, friction-max.
# gripper_offset=[0, 0, 0.01] — 10 mm hover (pen is only 16 mm diameter).
define_grasp_pose(
    robot_path="/World/Franka",
    object_path="/World/Pen",
    approach_direction=[0, 0, -1],
    gripper_offset=[0, 0, 0.01],
)

# ── Pick + transport: cuRobo carries the pen to target XY ───────────────────
# drop_targets pin the pen drop at the target XY (above the marker); the
# wrist orientation is locked by the grasp through transport — pen long-axis
# stays along world +X until the explicit reorient step below.
setup_pick_place_controller(
    robot_path="/World/Franka",
    target_source="curobo",
    source_paths=["/World/Pen"],
    destination_path="/World/PenTarget",
    drop_targets={"/World/Pen": [0.0, -0.40, 0.83]},
    planning_obstacles=[
        "/World/Table",
    ],
)

# ── Diagnostic read: confirm pen is at target XY before reorient ────────────
get_world_transform(prim_path="/World/Pen")

# ── Reorient: wrist roll to bring pen long-axis from +X to +Z ──────────────
# target_orientation [0.7071, 0, 0.7071, 0] = quaternion (w=cos(45deg), 0,
# y=sin(45deg), 0) = rotation +90 deg about world Y. Combined with the
# grasp transform, this rotates the pen long-axis from +X to +Z (vertical).
# planner='rmpflow' handles the in-air reorient — no obstacles in swept volume.
move_to_pose(
    articulation_path="/World/Franka",
    target_position=[0.0, -0.40, 0.93],
    target_orientation=[0.7071, 0.0, 0.7071, 0.0],
    planner="rmpflow",
    robot_type="franka",
)

# ── Final orientation check: read pen pose post-reorient ────────────────────
# The function-gate harness reads simulate_args.target_position_tolerance and
# simulate_args.target_orientation_axis_tolerance_deg to enforce success:
#   (a) pen XY within 0.05 m of [0.0, -0.40]
#   (b) pen long-axis dot +Z >= cos(5 deg) = 0.9962
# Both required for reorient pattern_hint.
get_world_transform(prim_path="/World/Pen")
failure_modes: Pen rolls during transport (cylindrical instability) — a Cylinder lying on its side has a line contact with the table (and a curved contact with the gripper fingers); any lateral acceleration imparts angular momentum around the long axis. If the cuRobo trajectory takes a curved path, the pen can roll within the gripper and exit the friction cone. Mitigation: define_grasp_pose with small gripper_offset (10 mm) so jaws close near contact + 'plastic'/'rubber' physics material (mu ~0.4); also pre-pinch with bulk_set_attribute lowering sleepThreshold to 0 so PhysX continuously tracks micro-rolls rather than caching a stale settled pose. Detection: get_world_transform before reorient — if pen orientation has drifted >5 deg around its long axis, log a regrasp event.

Grasp slip on curved surface — Franka parallel-jaw line contact on a 16 mm cylinder is geometrically thin (~2 mm Hertzian patch even with rubber pads). Franka grip force 70 N × friction 0.4 = 28 N hold, vs pen weight 0.012 × 9.81 = 0.12 N, so static slip is unlikely. But if the gripper closes off-axis (jaws not perpendicular to pen long axis), the contact patch shrinks to near-zero and the pen squirts out laterally during lift. Mitigation: define_grasp_pose's approach_direction=[0,0,-1] + the Franka default gripper Y-axis alignment perpendicular to world X (the pen long axis at pick time) produces the friction-max orientation. If a function-gate run shows slip, fall back to apply_physics_material 'rubber' on the pen + lower gripper closure speed via setup_pick_place_controller events_dt[3] increase.

Target orientation axis ambiguity — the pen has continuous rotational symmetry around its long axis (2-fold symmetry from the parallel-jaw perspective: which way the 'top' of the pen points around the axis doesn't matter for grasp or placement). Success metric must therefore compare ONLY the long-axis direction cosine, not the absolute quaternion. The simulate_args field target_orientation_axis=[0,0,1] + target_orientation_axis_tolerance_deg=5.0 encodes this: harness computes pen.long_axis_world · [0,0,1] and asserts dot >= cos(5 deg) = 0.9962, ignoring rotation around the axis. Failure mode is if the harness mistakenly compares full quaternions and reports false negatives on visually-correct end-states.

Tight-tolerance target pose conflicts with cuRobo approach direction — when move_to_pose is called with target_orientation=[0.7071, 0, 0.7071, 0] (gripper pointing down and rolled 90 deg), rmpflow IK may fail if the Franka elbow is in an awkward configuration after the cuRobo transport (e.g. elbow-up vs elbow-down ambiguity). Mitigation: if move_to_pose returns IK failure, chain a second move_to_pose with target_position lifted 0.10 m + same orientation (gives more elbow room), then drop. Detection: get_world_transform after the reorient — if pen Z < 0.78 or orientation axis-dot < 0.99, log a failed reorient and re-attempt.

Cylinder primitive radius/height attrs ignored by set_attribute — some Isaac Sim versions create a Cylinder prim with default UsdGeom.Cylinder schema where the radius/height authoring uses 'inputs:radius'/'inputs:height' OmniGraph variants rather than the raw schema attributes. If the pen renders as the default 1.0 m radius (because set_attribute silently failed to author), the scene becomes geometrically wrong and the gripper cannot fit. Mitigation: verify post-creation by reading get_world_transform on the pen — the BBox extent should reflect ~0.016 m × 0.016 m × 0.15 m. Fallback: re-create with size in create_prim itself, or use scale=[0.008, 0.008, 0.075] in create_prim (Cylinder scale is half-extents).

Pen final position misses target XY by >0.05 m — if cuRobo's drop_target landing is dominated by the controller's release phase (events 7-9 open the gripper, the pen falls under gravity), the pen may bounce sideways on impact with the table. The target_position_tolerance_m=0.05 captures a 5 cm XY hit-box around the marker. Mitigation: lower release height (move_to_pose target_position z=0.93 leaves the pen 0.10 m above table; reduce to 0.85 for a softer release), or chain a second move_to_pose at z=0.81 (almost-touching-table) before the gripper opens. Detection: get_world_transform after settle — if pen XY > 0.05 m from target, log a missed-target event.

RoboHive ADROIT-substitution boundary — this canonical uses Franka parallel-jaw, NOT the 24-DOF ADROIT dexterous hand that the source benchmark requires (asset_dependencies: ADROIT_hand_USD is Nucleus-only, blocker tagged in canonical_backlog.yaml). The substitution preserves the elongated-workpiece reorient signature but loses (a) finger-rolling regrasps mid-transport, (b) multi-contact stability that the dexterous hand affords, (c) the actual ADROIT relocate-policy comparison. structural_tags include both isaac:research.robohive (lineage) and isaac:robot.fixed_base.arm (actual implementation) — retrieval surfaces this template for 'pen relocate' queries but the user should understand the simplification.
code_template:
# ── Scene skeleton ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
create_prim(prim_path="/World/DomeLight", prim_type="DomeLight")
set_attribute(prim_path="/World/DomeLight", attr_name="inputs:intensity", value=1000.0)
create_prim(prim_path="/World/Ground", prim_type="Cube", position=[0, 0, -0.5], scale=[20, 20, 1])
apply_api_schema(prim_path="/World/Ground", schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")
create_prim(prim_path="/World/Cell", prim_type="Xform")
create_prim(prim_path={{table.path}}, prim_type="Cube", position={{table.position}}, scale={{table.scale}})
apply_api_schema(prim_path={{table.path}}, schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")

set_physics_scene_config(config={"enable_gpu_dynamics": False, "broadphase_type": "MBP"})

robot_wizard(
    robot_name={{primary_robot.class}},
    dest_path={{primary_robot.path}},
    position={{primary_robot.position}},
    orientation={{primary_robot.orientation}},
)

create_prim(
    prim_path={{workpiece.path}},
    prim_type="Cylinder",
    position={{workpiece.initial_position}},
)
set_attribute(prim_path={{workpiece.path}}, attr_name="radius", value={{workpiece.radius}})
set_attribute(prim_path={{workpiece.path}}, attr_name="height", value={{workpiece.height}})
set_attribute(prim_path={{workpiece.path}}, attr_name="xformOp:orient", value={{workpiece.initial_orientation}})
for api in ("PhysicsRigidBodyAPI", "PhysicsCollisionAPI", "PhysicsMassAPI", "PhysxRigidBodyAPI"):
    apply_api_schema(prim_path={{workpiece.path}}, schema_name=api)
set_attribute(prim_path={{workpiece.path}}, attr_name="physics:mass", value={{workpiece.mass_kg}})

bulk_set_attribute(prim_paths=[{{workpiece.path}}], attr="physxRigidBody:sleepThreshold", value=0.0)
bulk_set_attribute(prim_paths=[{{workpiece.path}}], attr="physxRigidBody:enableCCD", value=True)
apply_physics_material(prim_path={{workpiece.path}}, material_name="plastic")

create_prim(prim_path={{target_marker.path}}, prim_type="Xform", position={{target_marker.position}})
set_attribute(prim_path={{target_marker.path}}, attr_name="xformOp:orient", value={{target_marker.orientation}})

define_grasp_pose(
    robot_path={{primary_robot.path}},
    object_path={{workpiece.path}},
    approach_direction=[0, 0, -1],
    gripper_offset=[0, 0, 0.01],
)

setup_pick_place_controller(
    robot_path={{primary_robot.path}},
    target_source="curobo",
    source_paths=[{{workpiece.path}}],
    destination_path={{target_marker.path}},
    drop_targets={{'{'}}{{workpiece.path}}: {{target_marker.position}}{{'}'}},
    planning_obstacles=[{{table.path}}],
)

get_world_transform(prim_path={{workpiece.path}})

move_to_pose(
    articulation_path={{primary_robot.path}},
    target_position={{reorient_waypoint.position}},
    target_orientation={{reorient_waypoint.orientation}},
    planner="rmpflow",
    robot_type={{primary_robot.class}},
)

get_world_transform(prim_path={{workpiece.path}})
extension_notes: RoboHive ADROIT relocate-pen Isaac parity. SUBSTITUTION: Franka parallel-jaw replaces 24-DOF ADROIT dexterous hand (asset_dependencies ADROIT_hand_USD is Nucleus-only blocker per canonical_backlog.yaml). Preserves elongated-workpiece reorient signature + tabletop relocate shape; loses dexterous-finger contact richness. Future: when ADROIT_hand_USD becomes available, derive a CP-NEW-robohive-relocate-pen-adroit variant that swaps robot_wizard(franka) for the dexterous hand and replaces define_grasp_pose with a finger-rolling grasp script.
